refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and timely delivery.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and delivery to operational users, which are critical for productivity and safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios; verify data correctness and completeness; confirm update delivery to operational modules.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process sensor and telemetry data from vehicles and site equipment within 0.5 s.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to operational modules (e.g., Teleoperation, Safety Services) within 0.2 s.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time data ingestion and preprocessing pipeline with prioritization and buffering.
DP2: Centralized in-memory worksite model database with incremental update capability.
DP3: Event-driven publish-subscribe distribution mechanism for worksite updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to meet latency and accuracy requirements.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between data ingestion and model update timing.
Dependency of distribution mechanism on model update consistency.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interfaces and asynchronous event handling to minimize tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency is stable and within acceptable bounds.
Risk of data loss or delay under high load scenarios.
Assumes sufficient computational resources for real-time processing.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor system performance under load and consider fallback or degradation modes if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data ingestion, worksite model update, and distribution to operational modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and vehicle data streams for processing.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to operational modules for task execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and other modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary module containing all DP logic for worksite update; minimal diffusion.
Vehicle provides raw telemetry but does not contain update logic.
Consumes updated worksite model but does not implement update logic.
Consumes safety alerts; no containment of update logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports latency requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded.
Single point of failure risk for worksite update processing.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing internal components within NGHE Solution to distribute load and improve fault tolerance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear mapping and verification paths.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk as update logic is centralized in NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support strong ownership and minimal diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements and interface data flow monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update logic to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor system load to avoid bottlenecks in NGHE Solution.
Plan for fault tolerance and redundancy in update processing.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the latency and safety requirements with clear ownership and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process sensor and telemetry data from vehicles and site equipment within 0.5 s.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to operational modules (e.g., Teleoperation, Safety Services) within 0.2 s.

revise.why: Current level is sufficiently detailed and consistent; next step is to decompose sub-FRs.

assumptions: Network link latency remains stable and within bounds.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
open_risks: Potential overload of NGHE Solution module under peak load.
Single point of failure in centralized update logic.
evidence_needed: Performance testing data under realistic load scenarios.
Latency measurements for end-to-end worksite update.
Fault tolerance and recovery test results.
